ocr.text: The table presents the relationship between SNP frequency* and the number of Shandong gene models. For SNP frequency of
1, there are 1,119 gene models; for SNP frequency of 2, there are 854 gene models; for SNP frequency of 3, there are 590 gene
models; for SNP frequency of 4, there are 374 gene models; for SNP frequency of 5, there are 302 gene models; for SNP
frequency of 6, there are 179 gene models; for SNP frequency of 7, there are 122 gene models; for SNP frequency of 8, there
are 101 gene models; for SNP frequency of 9, there are 69 gene models; and for SNP frequency of ≥ 10, there are 310 gene
models. The total number of gene models across all SNP frequencies is 4,020. This data indicates the distribution of gene
models in relation to the frequency of SNP occurrences, showing a decreasing trend as the frequency increases, with the
maximum number of gene models observed at the lowest SNP frequency of 1. Each entry provides insights into the genetic
diversity present in the Shandong region, reflecting the varying degrees of SNP occurrences and their implications for genetic
research and breeding programs. The substantial total of 4,020 gene models highlights the richness of genetic material available
for study, emphasizing the importance of continued exploration in this area to uncover further genetic insights.
SNP frequency*  Number of Shandong gene models
